comment.body:
i think, i'm a bit over-explained.
as Rashid said not too long ago, I provide the same support/resistance levels, and some "checkpoints" like everyone else, but with a much higher accuracy (in terms of impact... like how many times the momentum/dynamics shits or gets confirmed after some levels) and I attach volatility expectations that sets the stage for the market movement (and i have a long post about what volatility is)...

there is no secret sauce to free lunch in terms to predict the next movement 100%

comment.body: Thank you very much for going into detail when I ask questions and need help. I know you do that consistently for other members, but there is an intimidation factor in asking questions that are so elementary when you know that you're surrounded by professionals with decades of experience. I'm not afraid to ask, although I'm taking up your time to explain things that I probably should already understand so I will go back and reread many of the educational posts because perhaps my failure to do that consistently is causing gaps in my knowledge. I agree with both you and Karen on strategy that is one of the things I'm struggling with right now is developing my own strategy and not relying on others. It's why I've never been able to stay in any discord, I need to understand it myself. I can't just blindly follow people. It's not in my nature.

comment.body:
1) this "dumb questions" thing comes from last april-may, when we had a lot of discord ppl who came after i predicted one of the biggest left-tail event since 2020, to date!! and downside target. 
and they asked things like "what is gamma???" and "up or down" literally every freaking day
those are dumb Qs
and ppl hate that

also:
don't ask things that are written in any posts.
bcs if i have to repeat my contents, then it is unnecessary to write emails collectively
and also don't ask about terms (like gamma, pnl etc.) bcs all of these can be googled: investopedia, optionseducation etc.
+ the books i recommended.

trading is a profession that should be learned. it won't just come to you
